evidence_quotes.quote: Right now publishers can sell you a game, then at any point they like decide to shut down the server that handles authentication and such, thus prohibiting you from enjoying the video game you paid for(see The Crew from Ubisoft).

paragraphs.text: I think video games themselves should be protected under this act. Right now publishers can sell you a game, then at any point they like decide to shut down the server that handles authentication and such, thus prohibiting you from enjoying the video game you paid for(see The Crew from Ubisoft). We're not asking for indefinite support and server uptime, that's impossible, but what they can do is provide us the resources to self-host such a server to enjoy the single player experience or even multiplayer on a local network(like having friends at your house).
